Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

content.text:
[Tue 2026-03-17 13:02 EDT] [Previous Feedback] You incorrectly selected option E: Incorrect - For consistency, even date-only fields should use the full datetime format (typically with 00:00:00 time)
Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

Please create day04/test_fixtures.json to provide test data for /tasks endpoint integration testing. Include 3 task records, each with id, title, created_at, updated_at, due_date, and completed_at (null for incomplete tasks) fields.

The user corrected me on option E - even date-only fields should use the full datetime format for consistency, typically with 00:00:00 time. So for due_date, it should be "2026-03-20T00:00:00+08:00" rather than just "2026-03-20".

Now I need to create day04/test_fixtures.json with 3 task records for /tasks endpoint integration testing. Each task should have:
- id
- title
- created_at
- updated_at
- due_date
- completed_at (null for incomplete tasks)

All datetime fields should use the full ISO 8601 format with +08:00 timezone.

Let me create 3 diverse task records with realistic timestamps.Understood. Even date-only fields should use the full datetime format with 00:00:00 for consistency.

The file was created successfully. Let me provide a summary to the user.Created `day04/test_fixtures.json` with 3 task records for /tasks endpoint integration testing:

| ID | Title | Created At | Updated At | Due Date | Completed At |
|----|-------|------------|------------|----------|--------------|
| task-001 | Complete API documentation | 2026-03-17T09:30:00+08:00 | 2026-03-18T14:20:00+08:00 | 2026-03-20T00:00:00+08:00 | null |
| task-002 | Fix authentication bug | 2026-03-15T11:00:00+08:00 | 2026-03-16T16:45:00+08:00 | 2026-03-18T00:00:00+08:00 | 2026-03-17T10:15:00+08:00 |
| task-003 | Update user dashboard | 2026-03-18T08:00:00+08:00 | 2026-03-18T08:00:00+08:00 | 2026-03-25T00:00:00+08:00 | null |

All datetime fields use the full ISO 8601 format with +08:00 timezone, including `due_date` which uses `T00:00:00+08:00` for consistency.
